revision.text:
[[ja:長者塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 長者塚古墳 |image = [[File:Chojazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |alternate_name = 関長者塚古墳 |所属 = |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|en|Kiryū, Gunma|qid=Q634022|lt=Kiryū, Gunma|1=Kiryū, Gunma}} Niisato-chō Seki 194 (aza Kamichōja) |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 18.45 |coordinates = 139|coordinates = 13|coordinates = 46.32 |omitted = JP-10 |type = Pentagonal tumulus |area = Diameter approx. 20m
Height approx. 2m |Burial Facility = Two-sided corridor-style {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (cut-stone joint stacking) |burial goods = |陪塚 = |built = Late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Designated municipal historic site “Chōjazuka Kofun” in Kiryū |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Gunma |アイコン = |ラベル = 長者塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Chōjazuka Kofun''' (長者塚古墳, also known as Seki Chōjazuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in the Niisato-chō Seki area of {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|en|Kiryū, Gunma|qid=Q634022|lt=Kiryū, Gunma|1=Kiryū, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a pentagonal tumulus and has been Historic Site1 as a municipal historic site by Kiryū City.{{Sfn|長者塚古墳（平凡社）|1987|qq=}} == Overview == This tomb was built on the southeastern slope of a tongue-shaped plateau (about 350 meters above sea level) at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|qid=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} in Gunma Prefecture, and it is located at the highest elevation among the kofun on Mount Akagi’s southern slopes.{{Sfn|長者塚古墳（平凡社）|1987|qq=}} It has been heavily damaged by grave robbers, and in recent years excavation surveys have been carried out. Although previously thought to be a circular tumulus, more recent research has revealed that it is pentagonal, measuring about 20 meters in diameter and about 2 meters in height. No {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been found on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/002/025/tyouzyatuka-kohun_hp.pdf 長者塚古墳詳細情報]|qq=}} (Kiryū City website). The burial facility is a two-sided corridor-style {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made with cut-stone joint stacking. Because the chamber was looted, any grave goods remain unknown. It is estimated to have been built in the {{ill|final stage of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final stage of the Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} in the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}. Designated in 1982 (Shōwa 57) by the typeer Niisato Village (now Kiryū), the site is recognized as a municipal historic site. About 3.7 kilometers to the south lie the {{ill|Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)|ja|中塚古墳 (桐生市)|lt=Nakazuka Kofun|lt_ja=中塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)|qq=}}, which also features a cut-stone joint-stacked stone chamber, and the octagonal tomb (a cremation grave) at the {{ill|Takei temple ruins|en|Takei temple ruins|qid=Q104878949|lt=Takei temple ruins|1=Takei temple ruins}}. == Excavation History == * {{ill|1958|en|1958|qid=Q5253|lt=1958|1=1958}} (Shōwa 33): Investigation by {{ill|Gunma University|en|Gunma University|qid=Q178017|lt=Gunma University|1=Gunma University}}.{{Sfn|長者塚古墳（平凡社）|1987|qq=}} * {{ill|1982|en|1982|qid=Q2436|lt=1982|1=1982}} (Shōwa 57) October 1: Designated as a historic site by the typeer Niisato Village (now Kiryū). * {{ill|2009|en|2009|qid=Q1996|lt=2009|1=2009}}–{{ill|2012|en|2012|qid=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}} (Heisei 21–24): Excavation surveys by the Kiryū City Board of Education (reported in 2012 and {{ill|2014|en|2014|qid=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}}). == burial == [[File:Chojazuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber|qq=}}{{small|Entrance corridor (foreground) and main chamber (background).|qq=}}]] A two-sided corridor-style {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} was used as the burial facility. Its area are as follows: * Main chamber: approx. 3.11–3.15 meters in length, 1.96 meters wide (center) * Entrance corridor: approx. 3.16–3.30 meters in length, 1.05 meters wide {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} was used for the stonework, arranged with cut-stone joint stacking. The entrance (“gateway”) is characterized by a type that flares outward at the bottom, resembling the kanji “八” (eight). File:Chojazuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Stonework of the west wall of the main chamber == Historic Site == === Kiryū City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Chōjazuka Kofun – Designated on October 1, 1982 (Shōwa 57).[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/shiseki/1002025.html 長者塚古墳] (Kiryū City website). == Footnotes == {{reflist|qq=}} == References == {{small|(References used in writing this article)|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=長者塚古墳|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} 10 Gunma Prefecture no Chimei|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|長者塚古墳（平凡社）|1987|qq=}}|qq=}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Related publications not used in writing this article)|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=関長者塚古墳II|title=桐生市内遺跡発掘調査報告 平成21･22nd year 度調査（桐生市文化財調査報告書 第30集）|publisher=桐生市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=|qq=}} ** 加部二生 “On the Kofun type of Seki Chōjazuka Kofun.” * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=関長者塚古墳III・関長者塚古墳IV|title=桐生市内遺跡発掘調査報告 平成23・24th year 度調査（桐生市文化財調査報告書 第33集）|publisher=桐生市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Chojazuka Kofun|qq=}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/shiseki/1002025.html Chōjazuka Kofun] – Kiryū City website {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|長者塚古墳|version=99995091|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:長者塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Chojazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q115498512}} [[Category:桐生市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Gunma Prefecture]]
